description:
## Background

Beadsmith currently renders a snapshot of the Current Workspace's Issue List. Beadwork can change after that snapshot through a local terminal, coding agent, or local `bw sync`; the app then silently shows stale Issues.

Beadwork's durable issue-data version is the commit SHA at the local `refs/heads/beadwork` ref. ADR-0007 chooses a 2-second probe of that ref over a filesystem watcher. This first vertical slice establishes the narrow, complete path from a local ref move to an updated Issue List while one Current Workspace remains selected.

The existing workspace transition protocol already owns Current Workspace selection and its generation. Refreshes inside that selection need a separate monotonic refresh revision: selection generation does not change every time the Beadwork ref moves.

## What to build

While a Current Workspace is selected, an external local change to its Beadwork ref must update All Issues, Ready, and Blocked in the running Beadsmith UI within roughly three seconds, without a manual refresh action or a disruptive loading state.

A user should be able to run `bw create`, `bw close`, or another local Beadwork mutation outside Beadsmith and see the resulting Issue List change in place. Rapid successive mutations must converge to the newest ref tip rather than leave the UI at an older snapshot.

## Technical Approach

- Add a refresh coordinator bound to the already-selected Current Workspace. It probes the fully qualified local ref through `git -C <workspace> rev-parse --verify refs/heads/beadwork^{commit}` every two seconds.
- Reuse the existing subprocess-runner test seam for the git probe and the existing Beadwork loaders for the full Issue List snapshot. Do not read Beadwork's git tree directly.
- Route ref-change requests through one non-overlapping loader. If a ref move is observed while a load is active, mark the request dirty and perform one follow-up load after the active one completes when the current ref still differs.
- Define the refresh event contract at the backend/frontend boundary. A successful event carries the full Issue Explorer data, the workspace path, the observed ref SHA, the existing workspace-selection generation, and a monotonically increasing refresh revision.
- Extend the renderer's existing admission behavior so it accepts only events for the current Workspace and only a revision newer than the currently rendered refresh revision. Do not create a parallel owner for Workspace selection.
- Preserve the existing initial-load behavior. This slice concerns automatic updates after a Current Workspace has a successful snapshot.

## Considerations

- Git refs can be loose or packed; the probe must ask `git` to resolve the ref instead of watching or parsing ref files directly.
- Several ref moves may occur within one two-second interval. The UI only needs to converge to the final local ref tip; it does not need to render every intermediate commit.
- A completion from an older refresh revision must never regress the Issue List after a newer snapshot has rendered.
- This task depends on the workspace-management epic because Current Workspace selection and its generation are already the ownership boundary for refreshes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With one Current Workspace selected, an external local `bw` mutation is reflected in All Issues, Ready, and Blocked within roughly three seconds.
- [ ] The automatic update keeps the existing Issue List visible while the refresh runs; it does not show the initial-load spinner, a toast, or a manual-refresh control.
- [ ] Two or more ref moves during an active load converge to the newest observed ref tip through the dirty follow-up load.
- [ ] Refresh events include workspace identity, workspace-selection generation, observed SHA, and refresh revision.
- [ ] The renderer rejects an event for another Workspace or an older refresh revision.
- [ ] The existing initial Issue List load continues to work unchanged.

## Test Plan

- Unit-test ref probing with the existing fake command runner: unchanged SHA, changed SHA, missing ref, and command failure.
- Unit-test the coordinator with controlled command outputs: no reload when SHA is unchanged, one reload on change, and dirty follow-up when the ref changes during a load.
- Unit-test renderer admission: a newer refresh revision wins and a late older event cannot regress the state.
- Add a slice-level WebDriver proof: launch the real desktop app, mutate Beadwork outside the app, and wait for the new Issue to appear without fixed sleeps.

comments.text:
# Implementation plan — bsm-wj1.1

## Goal

Make the Issue List for one already-selected **Current Workspace** converge automatically after the local `refs/heads/beadwork` ref moves. Every two seconds the backend asks Git for the ref tip, routes changes through one non-overlapping full Issue Explorer loader, updates the backend’s last good snapshot, and emits a full refresh event. The renderer admits only a refresh for the snapshot it is currently showing and only when its refresh revision is newer, then replaces All Issues, Ready, and Blocked in place.

The initial load remains the existing workspace/startup load. Automatic refresh never enters the initial loading presentation, remounts the Issue Explorer, shows a toast/banner, or adds a manual refresh control.

## Scope boundaries

This slice includes:

- a two-second probe of `refs/heads/beadwork^{commit}` through Git;
- a process-lifetime backend refresh coordinator that is idle without a Current Workspace;
- one full loader at a time, with a newest-dirty follow-up after ref movement during an active load;
- a success event containing the full Issue Explorer snapshot, Workspace path, Workspace-selection generation, observed ref SHA, and monotonic refresh revision;
- backend and renderer admission guards;
- a real-desktop proof driven by an external `bw` mutation.

This slice deliberately does **not** add the broader epic’s per-Workspace revisit cache and complete switch/remove/retry lifecycle behavior (`bsm-wj1.2`), refresh failure classification/banner and five-strike policy (`bsm-wj1.3`), or minute/focus triggers (`bsm-wj1.4`). Probe/load failures in this slice preserve the last good snapshot, are logged, and are retried by later probes.

## Design and invariants

### One backend module owns refresh orchestration

Add a `refresh` module with a small runtime interface and a testable coordinator implementation. The coordinator hides polling, single-flight state, dirty coalescing, revision allocation, and ref/SHA bookkeeping behind one start operation. Later timer and focus triggers must route into this same coordinator rather than create parallel loaders.

The coordinator’s state must enforce:

1. Git and Beadwork subprocesses never run while `WorkspaceRuntime`’s mutex is held.
2. At most one complete `load_issue_explorer_data` operation is active; All/Ready/Blocked remain one publication unit.
3. `last_published_sha` advances only after a successful load is still admissible for the bound Current Workspace and the backend snapshot has been replaced.
4. A changed SHA observed during a load replaces the dirty target with the newest observed SHA. After the active load finishes, immediately probe the ref again when dirty; run at most one follow-up for that **current** tip if it still differs from the published SHA. This avoids loading a transient dirty SHA that has already reverted.
5. The first successful probe for an unseeded coordinator triggers one silent refresh rather than merely establishing a baseline. This intentional duplicate of the startup load closes the race where the ref moves between the initial snapshot and the first probe; the delayed first tick prevents an event before renderer listeners exist.
6. Refresh revisions are monotonically increasing and independent of Workspace-selection generation. SHA is provenance, not an ordering token.
7. Failed, stale, discarded, or emit-failed loads do not advance the published SHA. The last good `WorkspaceRuntime.snapshot` remains available to later RPC reads. An emit failure may leave the backend snapshot newer than the renderer, so keeping the SHA retryable forces a later reload/re-emission.
8. No Current Workspace—or a Pending Workspace transition—means no new Git probe or Beadwork load.

Use `tokio::time::interval_at(now + 2s, 2s)` with `MissedTickBehavior::Skip`. The scheduler must continue probing while a blocking load is active (for example, select between poll ticks and the active loader completion); a loop that awaits the loader before polling cannot satisfy the dirty-follow-up requirement.

### Ref probe through the existing subprocess seam

Resolve the ref through the existing `CommandRunner` seam as:

```text
git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/beadwork^{commit}
```

with the Workspace supplied as `cwd`. This is equivalent to `git -C <workspace> ...`, keeps ADR-0006’s explicit path threading, and correctly handles loose refs, packed refs, and worktrees without parsing Git files.

A probe succeeds only for status 0 and trimmed, non-empty stdout. Do not hard-code SHA-1 length. Preserve spawn, non-zero status, and invalid-output detail internally so `bsm-wj1.3` can later classify failures without replacing the probe seam.

### Backend publication remains atomic with Current Workspace ownership

Reuse the existing `BeadsmithApiImpl`/`WorkspaceRuntime`; do not introduce another owner of Current Workspace or persist refresh state.

For each started load, capture an immutable binding `(workspace_path, workspace_selection_generation, observed_sha, refresh_revision)`. Load via `spawn_blocking`. On completion, briefly reacquire the runtime lock and verify the Current Workspace path and selection generation still match the binding. If they do:

1. replace `runtime.snapshot` with the complete new `IssueExplorerData`;
2. construct the frontend response/event from that same data and binding;
3. clone the `AppHandle`;
4. release the lock;
5. emit the event.

If identity no longer matches, discard the completion without touching the snapshot or published SHA. Publication returns an explicit outcome such as `Published`, `Discarded`, or `EmitFailed`; only `Published` advances the coordinator’s SHA. `EmitFailed` preserves the newer backend snapshot but leaves the SHA retryable so the running renderer is not stranded. This is the backend safety guard required by this slice; the fuller coordinator lifecycle across switching remains in `bsm-wj1.2`.

### Explicit success-event contract

Use event name `beadwork://issue-explorer-state-changed` and camelCase payload:

```ts
interface IssueExplorerRefreshEvent {
  issueData: LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse;
  observedRefSha: string;
  refreshRevision: number;
  workspacePath: string;
  workspaceSelectionGeneration: number;
}
```

`issueData` is the full All/Ready/Blocked snapshot. The Rust struct is the serialization source of truth; the renderer mirrors only the Tauri-event envelope, following the existing `workspace-transition` convention, while reusing the generated `LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse` for the nested snapshot. Add Rust serialization-shape coverage and a typed frontend fixture/helper to limit contract drift. Do not add a meaningless RPC solely to force a Rust-only Tauri event into TauRPC generation.

Extend `LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse` itself with `workspaceGeneration`, populated under the same runtime lock as `workspacePath` and the snapshot. Carry that field through `src/issues/issue-loader.ts` into successful `IssueExplorerData`; otherwise startup admission would silently drop the generation. This gives startup admission an atomic rendered identity `(path, selection generation)` instead of trying to reconcile the separately completing `load_issue_explorer_data` and `workspace_state` calls. Regenerate `src/rpc/bindings.ts`; never edit generated bindings manually. Existing switch/retry/cancel response construction must pass the corresponding `WorkspaceState.generation`.

### Renderer admission extends the existing transition gate

Do not weaken or reuse `applyWorkspaceTransition` for refresh events: its committed-generation guard correctly rejects same-generation Workspace-transition replays, while a valid within-selection refresh intentionally has the same selection generation.

Extend `WorkspaceTransitionGateState` with:

- `confirmedWorkspaceGeneration: number | null` paired with `confirmedWorkspacePath`;
- `acceptedRefreshRevision`, reset to its sentinel whenever a different snapshot identity is committed or the snapshot is cleared.

Add a pure `applyIssueExplorerRefresh` decision function beside `applyWorkspaceTransition`. Admit an event only when:

1. the gate has a confirmed rendered snapshot identity;
2. `workspacePath` matches the confirmed path;
3. `workspaceSelectionGeneration` matches the confirmed generation;
4. `issueData.workspacePath` and `issueData.workspaceGeneration` match the same identity;
5. `refreshRevision` is strictly greater than `acceptedRefreshRevision`.

On admission, advance only the refresh marker and return a `commitRefreshSnapshot` decision. Equal/older revisions and mismatched identities return `ignore` without mutating the gate.

Workspace snapshot commits (startup and successful Workspace transitions) establish the confirmed path/generation and reset refresh admission for that identity. A Pending transition retains the rendered identity and temporarily prevents backend refresh starts. When an admitted failure/cancellation ends Pending but retains the same Current path under a newly incremented selection generation, rebind the confirmed generation to that accepted generation without resetting/remounting the snapshot; otherwise valid post-failure refreshes would be rejected forever. Clearing Current clears both identity fields and resets refresh revision.

In `App`, subscribe to the refresh event and apply the pure decision. A committed refresh calls only `setIssueState({ ...event.issueData, status: "success" })`. It must not set `ISSUE_EXPLORER_LOADING_STATE`, update `workspaceKey`, change Workspace state, or create success/error presentation. Because the outer Issue Explorer key remains stable, active view, search, selected Issue, and scroll state remain in place while rows and sidebar counts rederive from the new snapshot.

Capture Tauri listeners by event name in App tests. Make startup subscription-first: await registration of both Workspace-transition and refresh listeners before dispatching initial snapshot/Workspace reads, with disposed/unlisten protection. The delayed first poll remains a secondary safeguard; do not rely on a missed event being replayed.

## Implementation sequence

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[1. Contract and probe] --> B[2. Coordinator reducer and scheduler]
  B --> C[3. Runtime publication and startup wiring]
  A --> D[4. Renderer admission gate]
  C --> E[5. App event integration]
  D --> E
  E --> F[6. Desktop mutation proof and docs]
  B --> F
  F --> G[7. Full verification]
```

### 1. Define the contract and Git probe

**Files:**

- Add `src-tauri/src/refresh.rs`
- Add `src-tauri/src/refresh/tests.rs`
- Modify `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
- Modify `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
- Regenerate `src/rpc/bindings.ts`

**Work:**

- Add constants for the fully qualified ref, event name, and two-second interval.
- Implement `probe_beadwork_ref(&dyn CommandRunner, &Path)` with exact program/args/cwd handling and typed internal errors.
- Define the immutable load binding and `IssueExplorerRefreshEvent` serialization shape.
- Add `workspace_generation` to `LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse` and update every response constructor/call site to pair data with the state generation that owns it.

**Tests:**

- exact Git program, args, and cwd;
- successful trimmed SHA;
- same/different SHA values are preserved for coordinator comparison;
- missing ref/non-zero status;
- spawn failure, including `NotFound` detail;
- successful command with empty output;
- event JSON uses the required camelCase fields and includes full Issue Explorer data.

### 2. Implement deterministic coordinator state before the async driver

**Files:**

- `src-tauri/src/refresh.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/refresh/tests.rs`

**Work:**

- Model idle, active-load, published SHA, newest dirty SHA, and next revision explicitly.
- Expose pure transitions for probe success, load success, load failure/stale completion, and follow-up scheduling.
- Keep the interface trigger-oriented so later time/focus sources can request refresh through the same module.
- Implement the async scheduler around the pure state, using one active `JoinHandle`, continued polling during load, delayed/skip-missed ticks, and `spawn_blocking` for all subprocess work.

**Tests (controlled outputs/clock; no real sleeps):**

- no Current Workspace produces no probe/load request;
- first observed SHA starts one load;
- unchanged published SHA starts no load;
- changed SHA starts exactly one load;
- repeated same SHA during a load does not create extra work;
- several different SHAs during a load retain only the newest dirty target and schedule exactly one immediate follow-up;
- successful publication advances SHA and revision;
- a dirty active load performs a post-completion probe and follows the current tip, including the case where a previously observed dirty SHA reverted;
- load failure, stale completion, or emit failure preserves published SHA so a later probe retries;
- a ref move between initial snapshot completion and the first probe is caught by the intentional first-probe refresh;
- never more than one complete Issue Explorer loader is active.

Use Tokio’s paused-time test support for cadence wiring, while keeping probe/load decisions in the pure coordinator tests.

### 3. Wire the coordinator into the existing Workspace runtime

**Files:**

- `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/rpc/tests.rs`

**Work:**

- Add a short runtime-binding helper that returns Current path + selection generation only when Current exists and Pending is absent; otherwise it is temporarily idle.
- Reuse/generalize `load_issue_explorer_data_outside_lock`; instantiate `ProcessRunner` inside blocking work rather than requiring test fakes to become `Send + Sync`.
- Add guarded publication that updates `runtime.snapshot` and creates the event in one short critical section, then emits after unlocking with structured logging on emit failure.
- Start exactly one process-lifetime refresh task in Tauri setup after `initialize_workspace` and before normal use. Avoid an immediate timer tick.

**Tests:**

- matching path/generation replaces the runtime snapshot and creates the complete event;
- mismatched path or generation discards completion and leaves snapshot unchanged;
- Pending Workspace suppresses new refresh work;
- event-emission failure leaves the new backend snapshot readable but does not advance the published SHA;
- the event carries the load binding’s observed SHA and revision;
- later `load_issue_explorer_data` reads the refreshed backend snapshot;
- existing initial load, switch, retry, cancel, removal, and generated-binding tests remain green.

### 4. Extend renderer identity and revision admission

**Files:**

- `src/workspaces/transition-gate.ts`
- `src/workspaces/transition-gate.test.ts`
- `src/issues/issue-loader.ts`
- `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts`
- `src/test/app-workspace-fixtures.ts`

**Work:**

- Add confirmed generation and accepted refresh revision to the existing gate.
- Carry generated `workspaceGeneration` through the Issue loader’s successful state and fixtures.
- Teach startup and successful Workspace snapshot commits to establish `(path, workspaceGeneration)` atomically.
- Preserve/reset those markers correctly for Pending, failed/cancelled selection generations that retain Current, replacement commits, and clears.
- Add the typed refresh envelope and pure `applyIssueExplorerRefresh` function.
- Update test listener/fixture helpers to identify event callbacks by event name.

**Tests:**

- matching newer revision is admitted;
- equal and older revisions are ignored without gate mutation;
- a newer event followed by a late older event cannot regress state;
- wrong Workspace path is rejected;
- wrong selection generation is rejected, including the same path revisited under another generation;
- inconsistent outer and nested snapshot identity is rejected;
- committing a new Workspace snapshot resets refresh admission;
- Pending retains the rendered identity, and failed/cancelled selection that retains the same Current path rebinds it to the new accepted generation without remounting;
- clearing Current clears the refresh identity;
- startup uses the generation carried with its snapshot and remains safe if the separate `workspace_state` RPC resolves before or after it.

### 5. Subscribe and update the Issue Explorer in place

**Files:**

- `src/App.tsx`
- Add `src/App.issue-refresh.test.tsx`
- Update listener mocks in `src/App.workspace-switching.test.tsx` and `src/App.workspace-recovery.test.tsx` as needed
- Optionally add focused rerender coverage in `src/issues/IssueExplorer.test.tsx` without changing production `IssueExplorer` code

**Work:**

- Register/clean up both Tauri listeners before dispatching startup reads through one subscription-first `useExternalLifecycle` flow (or an extracted helper with the same ordering guarantee).
- Route payloads through `applyIssueExplorerRefresh`.
- Replace only the successful `issueState` snapshot on admission.
- Leave Workspace ownership, remount key, initial loading/failure behavior, and Sidebar/IssueExplorer implementations unchanged.

**Tests:**

- a matching event atomically updates All, Ready, Blocked, and sidebar counts;
- the prior list stays visible before event completion and initial loading copy never appears;
- active view, search query, and selected same-ID Issue survive refresh while selected detail reflects new data;
- a removed/hidden selected Issue follows existing selection-clearing behavior;
- wrong path/generation and older revision events do nothing;
- adding the second event listener does not break Workspace transition listener behavior or cleanup;
- existing initial Issue List load behavior remains unchanged.

### 6. Add the real-desktop external mutation proof

**Files:**

- `e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts`
- `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts`
- `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.test.ts` if the helper interface changes
- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts`
- `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`
- `docs/adr/0007-refresh-issue-list-by-polling-beadwork-ref.md`

**Work:**

- Add a fixture helper that runs real external `bw create` commands and `bw dep add` in the already-selected disposable Workspace, returning generated IDs/titles.
- Put the proof in the existing `issues` scenario after baseline counts and before the later switch to empty Workspace B. Do not add a new scenario or package script.
- Make `expectSidebarCount` condition-wait for the requested changed label instead of reading an existing label once.
- From the WDIO process, create a Ready blocker and a target blocked by it. Wait by condition (no `browser.refresh`, `browser.pause`, fixed sleeps, or manual load RPC) until the final snapshot renders: All `6 → 8`, Ready `3 → 4`, Blocked `1 → 2`.
- Assert both new Issues appear in All, the blocker appears only in Ready, the target appears only in Blocked, an existing Issue remains visible during refresh, and no initial-load/full-pane failure presentation replaces the list.
- Log elapsed convergence time for diagnostics but use a CI-safe condition timeout rather than a brittle three-second hard cutoff.
- Update WebDriver documentation for external mutation coverage and `git` on PATH. Align ADR-0007’s event wording with the separate Workspace-selection generation + refresh revision contract.

The real rapid mutations demonstrate eventual convergence to the final ref tip. The deterministic coordinator unit test—not wall-clock WebDriver timing—proves the dirty-during-active-load branch.

## Verification

Run focused checks during implementation, then the full gates:

```sh
cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml refresh
cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml rpc
pnpm exec vitest run src/workspaces/transition-gate.test.ts src/App.issue-refresh.test.tsx
pnpm test
cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml
pnpm check
pnpm e2e:build
pnpm e2e:issue-list:success
pnpm test:e2e:issue-list
```

`pnpm test:e2e:issue-list` is the final desktop gate because it rebuilds and repeats Issue List scenarios in normal, reverse, and normal order, protecting the existing store-isolation guarantees.

## Acceptance-criteria trace

- **External mutations update All/Ready/Blocked within roughly three seconds:** two-second Git polling plus full-loader event path; real desktop condition-wait proof records elapsed time.
- **No disruptive refresh presentation:** backend preserves the last snapshot; App refresh handler only replaces success data; deterministic App test plus desktop sampling.
- **Rapid changes converge:** continued probing during one active load, newest-dirty coalescing, immediate follow-up; controlled coordinator test and final-state desktop proof.
- **Complete event identity:** Rust serialization test covers Workspace path, selection generation, observed SHA, refresh revision, and full data.
- **Renderer rejects wrong Workspace/old revision:** pure gate tests cover path, generation, nested identity, equal/older revision, and out-of-order delivery.
- **Initial load unchanged:** existing startup path remains authoritative and its current tests plus the first desktop test stay green; only its response gains the generation needed for atomic admission.

## Risks and mitigations

- **Three sequential `bw` calls are not a Git transaction.** A ref may move between views. Continued polling and the dirty follow-up provide eventual convergence; do not describe an individual refresh snapshot as ref-atomic.
- **A two-second probe plus three loaders may exceed three seconds on slow CI.** Keep the production cadence fixed, use condition waits with diagnostic elapsed timing, and treat “roughly” as a product budget rather than a brittle test deadline.
- **Tauri events are lossy during renderer startup.** Delay the first tick, register listeners promptly, and update the backend snapshot before emission so later RPC reads cannot regress.
- **Rust-only Tauri event envelopes are not automatically TauRPC-generated.** Keep Rust serialization canonical, reuse the generated nested response, mirror one small frontend envelope, and test its shape on both sides.
- **This slice lands before full switch lifecycle hardening.** Guard every publication and renderer admission by path + existing selection generation now; leave per-Workspace cache, cancellation/revisit behavior, and expanded switch races to `bsm-wj1.2`.

title: Refresh Current Workspace when the Beadwork ref changes
